seoTitle: How to Add an Equipment Loan to QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Set up an equipment loan in QuickBooks Online step by step to track payments, interest, and balances accurately.
intro: Tracking an equipment loan properly matters for businesses relying on machinery or tools, since it affects both cash flow planning and financial reporting accuracy. This guide walks through setting one up in QuickBooks Online. It's aimed at small business owners, accountants, and financial advisors managing loan obligations.

steps.heading: Review the equipment loan details
steps.body: Gather the loan amount, terms, and payment schedule before entering it into QuickBooks.

steps.heading: Access QuickBooks Online
steps.body: Navigate to the section where liability accounts or loans are managed.

steps.heading: Add the loan details
steps.body: Enter the loan as a liability account with the correct balance and terms.

steps.heading: Review and finalize
steps.body: Double-check the entered information before saving to make sure it reflects the loan accurately.

faq.question: Where does an equipment loan show up in QuickBooks reports?
faq.answer: It typically appears as a liability on your balance sheet once entered correctly.

faq.question: Can I track loan payments over time in QuickBooks?
faq.answer: Yes, recording payments against the loan account lets you monitor the remaining balance over time.

faq.question: Does QuickBooks calculate loan interest automatically?
faq.answer: Interest handling depends on how the loan account is set up; some setups require manual interest entries.
